summary: Bug fix verification for real-world CSV header incompatibility. Added 5 new pytest cases + kept all 14 previous tests as regression. All 19/19 tests pass. Confirmed: user's real processor-flavored CSVs (headers: transaction_ref/processed_at/order_reference/amount/type + status=settled) now upload with 200 and produce expected counts (orders=185, payments=187, MATCHED=167, REFUND_MISMATCH=1, plus AMOUNT_MISMATCH=3, CURRENCY_MISMATCH=2, DUPLICATE_PAYMENT=3, STATUS_CONFLICT=2, CANCELLED_BUT_PAID=1, ORPHAN_PAYMENT=5, MISSING_PAYMENT=6; total_money_at_risk_usd=$2,554.37). Regression with /app/sample_data/ still yields 10 orders / 9 payments / all 9 discrepancy types + MATCHED / ~$865.10. Header validation with only 'foo,bar' still returns 400 listing canonical names (order_id, net_amount, ...).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:143-161 ORDERS_ALIASES/PAYMENTS_ALIASES - well-designed, case-insensitive resolution via _resolve_aliases; note that 'amount' is aliased both as gross_amount for orders AND paid_amount for payments — safe because per-file alias maps, but worth noting for future maintenance.

server.py:194-214 _normalize_payment_status - correctly maps type=refund + status=settled → refunded with negative amount; also handles status=refunded alone with sign-flip only when amount was positive (preserves already-negative refund rows).
server.py:302 - _validate_upload is still called AFTER await file.read() (pre-existing minor concern from iter_1, not part of this bug-fix). Non-blocking.

server.py:316-321 - 400 responses correctly list canonical header names (e.g. 'orders.csv missing headers: [\'net_amount\', \'order_id\', ...]') when no alias matches. Verified via test_upload_totally_missing_canonical_headers.
test_credentials: Existing e2e@test.com/testpass123 available. Tests self-provision fresh users via /api/auth/signup.
seed_data_creation: Copied user's real files from /tmp/u_orders.csv and /tmp/u_payments.csv into /app/user_samples/ (185 orders, 187 payments).
context_for_next_testing_agent: backend_test.py now has 19 tests. Real-user CSV lives at /app/user_samples/. Expected counts on real CSVs: orders=185, payments=187, MATCHED=167, REFUND_MISMATCH=1, total_money_at_risk_usd~=$2554.37. Header aliasing lives in server.py ORDERS_ALIASES/PAYMENTS_ALIASES (~L143-161) and status vocab in STATUS_*_ALIASES + _normalize_payment_status (~L188-214). Frontend was not retested — flow already validated in iter_1.
rca of the issue: Original bug: /api/runs rejected the user's payments.csv with 400 'missing headers: [method, order_id, paid_amount, payment_date, payment_id]' because create_run compared raw CSV headers against canonical names only. Fix in server.py introduces (a) ORDERS_ALIASES/PAYMENTS_ALIASES mapping canonical→[aliases] including processor-flavored names (transaction_ref, processed_at, order_reference, amount, type, ...); (b) _missing_canonical_headers() checks if ANY alias is present before rejecting; (c) _resolve_aliases() rewrites rows to canonical keys before normalization; (d) STATUS_SUCCESS_ALIASES includes 'settled/captured/paid/completed' so payment_status=settled matches order.completed; (e) _normalize_payment_status flips sign to negative when type=refund + status=settled so REFUND_MISMATCH triggers vs refunded orders. Verified end-to-end via pytest (19/19 pass) and direct API probe (MATCHED=167, REFUND_MISMATCH=1).
